input.background: Consider a communication network of having 10 stations where the repair time for each station, in case a station is failed, follows the exponential distribution with $\beta=19 \mathrm{~h}$. If 10 stations have failed simultaneously and one repair person is available.
input.question: What is the probability that communication failure will last more than $45 \min$?

output.answer: This problem can be formulated using a gamma distribution (see Eq. 2.77) with $\alpha=10$ and $\beta=19$. Thus (45 min is same as 0.75 h), $$\begin{aligned} P(T>0.75) & =1-P(T \leq 0.75) \\ & =1-\int_0^{.75} \frac{(19)^{10} t^9}{(10-1)!} e^{-19 t} d t \approx 0.11 \end{aligned}$$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
